supported_schema_policy_runtime: destructive type-change block with failed schema evidence
declared-schema GCS load source probe inspection
decision.planner_behavior: Render and record type changes as schema evidence, but do not emit or execute BigQuery type-mutation DDL automatically.
decision.runtime_behavior: Block type changes under the current GCP smoke runtime unless a future explicitly certified mutation path is added.

rationale: ContractForge schema policy must remain portable across adapters. BigQuery supports some schema changes, but automatic BigQuery type widening or type mutation has platform-specific safety and rewrite implications.
The validated GCP behavior is to apply nullable additions and block type changes with persisted schema evidence.
Future type-widening support can be added only as an explicit, evidence-backed adapter extension with reviewed DDL, replay proof and readback.
required_before_future_promotion: Define the exact BigQuery type-widening matrix accepted by ContractForge for GCP.
Render reviewed BigQuery DDL or table-rewrite plans for each accepted widening.
Validate successful widening and rollback/failure evidence in a real BigQuery project.
Prove replay idempotency and downstream read compatibility for widened columns.
